# The Anatomy of a Venture Capital Term Sheet in 2026

## Summary
2026 term sheet landscape analysis. The market standard for Series A liquidation preference is 1x non-participating preferred, allowing investors to either recover their investment or convert to common at their ownership percentage. Participating preferred (investors get preference AND proportional share of remaining proceeds) significantly reduces founder payouts in modest exits. Weighted-average anti-dilution is the market standard; full ratchet is rarely used. Pro-rata rights, information rights, and co-sale/first refusal rights are standard package. The 2026 environment reflects a swing back toward investor-protective terms after the founder-friendly 2021 era.

## Key quotations / statistics
- "The market standard for Series A is a 1x non-participating preference, which allows investors to either recover their initial investment or convert to common shares based on their ownership percentage"
- "Participating preferred stock...significantly reduces founder payouts in modest exits and should be avoided if possible"
- "The weighted-average formula is the standard approach and balances investor protection with founder interests"
- "Full ratchet protection...is rarely founder-friendly and rarely requested by investors"
- "Preferred stockholders receive standard broad-based weighted average anti-dilution rights, first refusal and co-sale rights over founder stock transfers, registration rights, pro-rata rights and information rights"
- "Founders tend to over-negotiate valuation while under-negotiating liquidation preference, anti-dilution, and pre-money option pool—the three clauses that most destroy founder value when mis-negotiated"
- "The 2026 term sheet landscape reflects a swing back toward more balanced terms favoring investor protections after the founder-friendly period of 2021"

## Annotations for stinger-forge
- This is the primary source for `guides/03-priced-round-mechanics.md`. The "three value-destroying clauses" (liquidation preference, anti-dilution, pre-money option pool) framing is a memorable structure for the guide.
- The "1x non-participating" vs "participating preferred" distinction must be explained with a worked numerical example to make the founder impact concrete.
- YC Series A Term Sheet (https://ycombinator.com/series_a_term_sheet) was found in the search results as an official source - this should be scraped separately as the canonical template reference.
- The 2026 market shift toward investor-protective terms is a timely data point that contextualizes why founders need to understand these provisions more carefully in 2026 than in 2021-2022.
- The Command Brief open question about "most common investor data-room platforms" is partially answered here: data room is a separate concern from term sheet mechanics.
